brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (B) with valid core argument about bijection impossibility; only surface-level analysis — states the bijection barrier without exploring structural differences like element orders or why cyclic family membership doesn't imply isomorphism; no explicit monitoring of reasoning process or acknowledgment of potential confusion points; no counterfactual checks or verification from an alternative angle (e.g., order-of-elements argument); presents conclusion as certain with no acknowledgment of what assumptions underlie the argument; mentions alternatives A/C only implicitly by noting 'cyclic is a family,' without directly addressing why D is wrong or why C's reasoning fails; the bijection-to-conclusion chain is traceable but skips justifying why isomorphism requires bijection.
